text: From where the sun now stands I will fight no more forever.
context: Ending the Nez Perce fighting retreat.

text: Hear me, my chiefs! I am tired; my heart is sick and sad.
It is cold, and we have no blankets; the little children are freezing to death.
context: Naming the human cost of pursuit.

text: I want to have time to look for my children, and see how many of them I can find. Maybe I shall find them among the dead.
Treat all men alike. Give them all the same law. Give them all an even chance to live and grow.
Good words do not last long unless they amount to something.
I believe much trouble and blood would be saved if we opened our hearts more.
We only ask an even chance to live as other men live.
